reviews.review_description:
The game is a real failure :
TONS of bugs (Hand not detected by the game, cannot grab the ball, no "flashlight" is emerging from hands)
 No tutorial on how to play the "game" 
doesn't show everything the Quest and Passthrough are capable of (Windows not featured in the game, as of couch and offices)
 voice recognition is absolutely terrible if you're not native English (Google's Speech-To-Text understands me 100% of the time, but this is really difficult for the Quest)
Literally the game with the least playtime I ever saw, even on PC (count around 5 real minutes to finish it, and that's not like Firewatch where you can explore more after your first play)
Even when you haven't finished it, this is really boring : There is no game. The only things you can do, is to : 1) Launch a ball to make the creature give it back to you 2) Pet the creature
The Quest is capable of so much more, and The World Beyond was designed to showcase everything possible with Passthrough, but sadly, even some games that are almost proof of concepts downloadable via third party apps with hand tracking are quite better than this.

So you pick up a tool, you look for energy balls and suck them up.  Some annoying critter pops up and you can say hi, come, or jump to it.  You point the ray at a wall and it opens a portal.  You can spit the balls into the portal and the critter goes and eats them.  That's it.  I certainly hope it's way more engaging this.  It was extremely pointless and underwhelming compared to the rest of the VR experience.

Setup my room, put probably five objects in the room and the game just glitches out and barely runs. i realise this is experimental, but i feel like the game should work before putting out to the public.
To play the game u need to have devloper mode on i wasted like 30min

I downloaded it yesterday and played it with NO PROBLEM... But today it updated, and now when I go to grab the little gun thingy, it all disappears... I can HEAR the little alien, but the only thing I see is my room 🤷

I can't tell if I am holding the gun thing or not as I CAN'T see any animations...
it has A lot of worlds in it!

As someone who is always on the lookout for new and exciting virtual reality experiences, I was intrigued by The World Beyond app for my Oculus Quest 2. The concept of merging my play space with a virtual landscape and interacting with a character using my hands and voice was appealing.

Upon launching the app, I was disappointed to encounter a black screen and various electronic chirping sounds. I tried resetting my device and redrawing my room, but had no luck. I eventually discovered that turning on developer mode in the Oculus mobile app fixed the issue for me, but others have reported continued problems.

Once the app was working, I was able to use a magic flashlight to find energy orbs in my own room, hidden behind the passthrough feature. I used my hands to grab and toss the orbs, and even used voice commands to ask the character, Oppy, to perform actions. While the app was short, lasting only a few minutes, it was a fun taste of what mixed reality experiences could offer in the future.

Overall, while The World Beyond had some technical issues, it was a unique and enjoyable VR app that showed the potential of mixed reality. I hope to see more developers take inspiration from it and create more fully fleshed out experiences in the future.

--

If you want to purchase additional headsets for family members, loved ones, or friends, you can use the €30 credit available through the coupon code link (https://www.oculus.com/referrals/link/xinwix/) to get a discount.

Okay I'm think is good and is free but they are usually not what is coming Is like a really nice sorprise every day they showing you something because I was like a little bit nervous before I still a little bit with the game award for best games for talking about this is awesome

reviews.review_title: It's cool, but also very short
reviews.review_description: Even after doing the full set up at the latest software version it still won't work, it worked on a previous version, and the version before that didn't work either. You'd think a company with a several billion dollar budget would be capable of delivering something that at least functions properly. Like the developers, I never finished the game.

reviews.review_title: It's just a Demo and that's ok
reviews.review_description:
It does what it's supposed to do and that's show off the AR and mixed Reality stuff the Quest can do.

It's simple but yet special seeing it work the first time. Of cource it's short but hey, it's free. Takes 5 minutes to expirience something you likely haven't before so yeah.

Only 4 Stars because I think there could be some small things to add like some more enviroments to watch into + It is a little janky sometimes, which can break immersion.
I can’t get rid of the furniture borders don’t get this game unless you want to spend a lot of time trying to get rid of them

I was excited to see this ..however.. nothing but a black screen as others have stated .. in order to use Dev mode u  have to sign up ..add a credit card phone number etc... I'm not doing all that ... Don't advertise it for public use if you have to jump through all kinds of hoops to get it to work ... Geeez wtf
This is a cool game
Just tried to play. Nothing but black
I can’t play my  game anymore it turns off and on
FIX NOW

It works for me. Though, as cool as this tech demo is... I find it to be tragically ironic.

It is meant to show us how amazing and good Mixed reality is... But all it showed me instead was that I genuinely dislike being reminded that im in my room, when all I really want is to escape into something nicer.... like a forest... like the forest that you see through your walls in this demo... yet the moment you look down, bam. its your room's floor, a stain amongst the beautiful green grass, trees and flowers around you

Ignore all the reviews from people who simply just need to activate dev mode on their device. This fixes the black screen issue most of the time. 
Not long enough but an impressive display of what’s to come
Just got the game and it's just a black screen with that chirp sound (basically everyone else's problem) Zuckerberg fix. It!
Great demonstration of what you can do with passthrough even if it's only few mins long.
